vocab.meaning: làm chật chội/lộn xộn túi quần áo
vocab.example: I rarely carry a physical mirror because I prefer not to clutter my pockets with unnecessary items.
I usually keep a small, folding mirror discreetly tucked away in the inner compartment of my briefcase.

vocab.meaning: loại bỏ sự cần thiết của cái gì
vocab.example: Modern smartphones have high-resolution cameras that effectively obviate the need for carrying a traditional glass mirror.

vocab.meaning: một phụ kiện riêng biệt (không đi kèm thứ khác)
vocab.example: I don't view a mirror as a standalone accessory worth carrying when my powder foundation case already has one built-in.

vocab.meaning: chiếm diện tích không đáng kể
vocab.example: The ultra-thin mirrors made of polished steel occupy negligible space, making them ideal for minimalist travelers.

vocab.meaning: cực kỳ cầu kỳ/cẩn thận về ngoại hình
vocab.example: People who are fastidious about their appearance often find it vital to have a reflective surface handy at all times.
I avoid taking a mirror with me because the glass is prone to breakage if my bag gets jostled in a crowd.

vocab.meaning: phản hồi hình ảnh tức thì
vocab.example: Nothing beats a real mirror for providing instantaneous visual feedback when I need to fix my contact lenses.

vocab.meaning: tối giản hóa những vật dụng mang theo hàng ngày
vocab.example: In an effort to streamline my daily carry, I’ve started relying solely on digital screens instead of physical mirrors.

vocab.meaning: sự tự tin về diện mạo/trang phục
vocab.example: Having a mirror in my bag provides a sense of sartorial self-assurance before I walk into an important presentation.
